mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior quarter, discount rates and recession fear both ease, so S&P 500 prices should rise over the next month as investors reprice future cash flows higher.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of periods with a declining 3-month FEDFUNDS trend, or if the rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 55%, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent range signals weakening household income and demand, which should pressure corporate revenues and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the average forward return is not negative in at least two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly retail sales growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer demand and revenue momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is not associated with above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears in any rolling 2-year window with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in INDPRO that is not matched by higher inflation indicates improving real activity without immediate price pressure, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL 3-month change stays below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO with subdued CPI inflation do not outperform unconditional 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term becomes insignificant in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in HOUST above its recent baseline signals stronger housing demand and construction activity, which should improve cyclical earnings expectations and push S&P 500 higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakout periods are followed by negative or flat 60-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the average return advantage versus non-breakout periods is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is high and CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated, tighter real financial conditions should compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation regime does not produce below-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in PAYEMS growth and increase in UNRATE indicates deteriorating labor demand, which should weaken earnings forecasts and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change turns negative while UNRATE rises
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor deterioration regime does not underperform the sample average on a forward 20-day basis, or if positive SPX returns still occur in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDPC1 growth combined with soft RSAFS sales indicates broad demand deceleration, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as revenue and margin expectations are marked down.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth below 2% and RSAFS 3-month growth below 0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weak-growth regime does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns relative to unconditional performance, or if the effect vanishes in walk-forward tests with Rank IC below 0.03.
